text:
Bertolt Weill is a dramatist and one of the two protagonists of Bahamut, the ninth story of the Attack on Titan Anthology.

Appearance

Bertolt has tanned skin and black hair. He wears blue sunglasses, a pink scarf, with a white shirt and brown pants.

Personality

Bertolt appears to be a carefree and confident person, as he shows no concern when the police burst in his house and seek illegal things. Despite the success of his plays, he does not show arrogance but somehow seems elusive. Bertolt also appears to be tolerant, as he did not really mind his lover, Sophie, who could turn into a Titan. However, he was willing to abandon her when the Survey Corps found him with her, as he would be regarded as a traitor.

Story

After the success of his play, Bertolt exits the theater and gives D.S. Winters flowers he got earlier. He meets his sponsors who praise him for his success then goes to a party. Later on, the police busts in his home, looking for drugs. Bertolt calmly lets them do as they wished, then D.S. Winters arrives and takes care of the case.

Bertolt is later seen being interviewed by reporters. He then goes shopping and meets his lover Sophie. They go to the forest and have a picnic then sex. Sophie then shows him she can turn into a Titan. Later at night, they are awakened by a Survey Corps squad. Panicking, Bertolt claims he has been kidnapped, which angers Sophie. The Survey Corps make her go away and arrest Bertolt for treason.

The day of his execution, Bertolt is led to be hanged in public but Titans suddenly arrive in the town. Bertolt sees Sophie, who saves him from being killed by Makō Messer and exits the town with him. She releases him and Bertolt reminds her of their relationship but she abandons him, much to his dismay.
